paper_metadata.title: Using DeepSpeed and Megatron to Train Megatron-Turing NLG 530B, A Large-Scale Generative Language Model
paper_metadata.abstract: Pretrained general-purpose language models can achieve state-of-the-art accuracies in various natural language processing domains by adapting to downstream tasks via zero-shot, few-shot and fine-tuning techniques. Because of their success, the size of these models has increased rapidly, requiring high-performance hardware, software, and algorithmic techniques to enable training such large models. As the result of a joint effort between Microsoft and NVIDIA, we present details on the training of the largest monolithic transformer based language model, Megatron-Turing NLG 530B (MT-NLG), with 530 billion parameters. In this paper, we first focus on the infrastructure as well as the 3D parallelism methodology used to train this model using DeepSpeed and Megatron. Next, we detail the training process, the design of our training corpus, and our data curation techniques, which we believe is a key ingredient to the success of the model. Finally, we discuss various evaluation results, as well as other interesting observations and new properties exhibited by MT-NLG. We demonstrate that MT-NLG achieves superior zero-, one-, and few-shot learning accuracies on several NLP benchmarks and establishes new state-of-the-art results. We believe that our contributions will help further the development of large-scale training infrastructures, large-scale language models, and natural language generations.

extraction_data.innovation: Large-scale generative language model trained with DeepSpeed and Megatron, demonstrating improved performance on natural language inference and other tasks through careful shot selection and distribution tuning.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Pile, OpenWebText2, Wikipedia, Books3, StackExchange, PubMedAbstracts, Gutenberg, NIHExPorter, ArXiv, GitHub, Pile-CC, CC-2020-50, CC-2021-04, RealNews, CC-Stories
extraction_data.optimizer: Adam (assumed based on typical use in transformer models; paper does not specify)
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs and HDR InfiniBand networking
extraction_data.extension: The model's performance can be significantly improved by controlling the distribution of shot examples and adjusting the decision threshold, counteracting inherent biases and improving generalization.
extraction_data.license: Closed source (as per paper's mention of collaboration between Microsoft and NVIDIA)
extraction_data.research_problem: Large Language Models, Natural Language Understanding, In-context Learning
extraction_data.application: Natural Language Understanding, Question Answering, Code Generation, Riddle Solving, Mathematical Reasoning
